problem: The CLI's 'hlx comments post' command unconditionally sets isHelixTagged=true on every comment (post.ts:33), regardless of who is posting. This means any external user commenting via the CLI wrongly has their comment marked as Helix-tagged. Additionally, the CLI stores no user identity information — only apiKey and url — so there is no local signal to determine whether the current user is a Helix sandbox agent or an external human user.

files.why: Line 33: hardcoded { content: message, isHelixTagged: true } — always tags comments as Helix regardless of who posts.
Comment list display: isAgentAuthored → shows 'Helix' as author (line 48-49). Filters: --helix-only (isHelixTagged), --since (date).
Comments subcommand router — dispatches to list/post, resolves ticketId from args or HELIX_TICKET_ID env var.
Auth config: env vars HELIX_API_KEY/HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN take priority over ~/.hlx/config.json. Stores only { apiKey, url } — no user identity.
HTTP client: hxi_ keys sent via X-API-Key header, others via Bearer. Supports configurable basePath parameter.
OAuth browser flow + manual mode. Saves { apiKey, url } to ~/.hlx/config.json. No user ID, name, or agent type stored.
CLI entry point — routes 'comments' subcommand with ticketId from HELIX_TICKET_ID env var or --ticket flag.

facts: hlx comments post hardcodes isHelixTagged: true (post.ts:33) — every CLI-posted comment is Helix-tagged regardless of who posts.
hlx comments list already supports --helix-only and --since filters (list.ts:31-39).
CLI config stores only { apiKey, url } (config.ts:6-8) — no user identity, no agent type flag.
Env var priority: HELIX_API_KEY > HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN > HELIX_INSPECT_API_KEY for apiKey (config.ts:15).
Sandbox agents get HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN and HELIX_INSPECT_BASE_URL env vars from orchestrator (config.ts:15-16 reads these).
HELIX_TICKET_ID env var is available in sandbox and used by comments/index.ts to resolve ticketId.
hxFetch supports configurable basePath — comment commands use basePath: '/api' (post.ts:34, list.ts:26).
hxi_ keys use X-API-Key header; other tokens use Authorization: Bearer header (http.ts).
Build/quality: npm run build (tsc), npm run typecheck (tsc --noEmit). No lint or test commands.
Zero runtime dependencies; CLI binary is 'hlx'.
unknowns: Whether isHelixTagged on post should be conditionally set based on some signal (e.g., detecting sandbox agent context via HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN env var, or server-side determination).
Whether the CLI should stop sending isHelixTagged entirely and let the server determine it based on the auth identity.
Whether the CLI login flow should store user identity (name/email/id) in config so it can be displayed or sent with requests.
